steps.title: Use the parallel sides
steps.content: Extend $BC$ and $DE$ until they meet at point $X$. Since $AB\parallel DE$, the exterior angle at $B$ and angle $CXD$ are alternate interior angles.

steps.title: Find the two angles in triangle CDX
steps.content: The exterior angle at $B$ is $180^{\circ}-136^{\circ}=44^{\circ}$, so $\angle CXD=44^{\circ}$. Also, $\angle CDX=180^{\circ}-171^{\circ}=9^{\circ}$.

steps.title: Use the exterior angle theorem
steps.content: Angle $C$ of the pentagon is the exterior angle of triangle $CDX$, so its measure is the sum of the two remote interior angles: $$44+9=53.$$
